steps.title: Find the required preposition
steps.content: The sentence reads: "The element carbon has the highest melting point ______ all the elements on the periodic table." The superlative phrase "highest melting point" needs a preposition to introduce the group being compared. In standard English, "highest X of [group]" is the correct structure — "of" links the superlative to its comparison group.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'of': "of" directly joins "highest melting point" to "all the elements" with no intervening punctuation — grammatically clean. ✓
  • Choice B — 'of—': the em dash separates the preposition from its object, breaking the required connection. ✗
  • Choice C — 'of,': the comma between the preposition and its object violates standard usage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'of:': a colon cannot come between a preposition and its object. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
